input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
validate-catalog: use hasOwnProperty.call for equipment lookups

The `in` operator includes inherited properties from `Object.prototype`
(toString, constructor, etc), which would let an exercise reference one
of those names without the validator flagging it.
EOF
)" && git push

content.text: Now let me reply to both review comments.

input.command: gh api repos/gunk-dev/flux/pulls/84/comments/3326346599/replies -f body='Good catch — switched to `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(equipmentCatalog, kind)` so inherited `Object.prototype` keys (toString, constructor, etc) cannot sneak past the equipmentRequired check.'

gh api repos/gunk-dev/flux/pulls/84/comments/3326346617/replies -f body='Applied the same fix to the equipmentAlternatives branch — both equipment-kind checks now use `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call` so inherited keys cannot match.'

content.text: Both review comments addressed. Both flagged the same prototype-pollution concern: `kind in equipmentCatalog` would match inherited `Object.prototype` keys (`toString`, `constructor`, etc.). Switched both equipment-kind checks (in `equipmentRequired` and `equipmentAlternatives`) to `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(equipmentCatalog, kind)`. Tests still pass (13/13 in `programSchema.spec.ts`); pushed as `d8fe56d` and replied to each comment.

result: Both review comments addressed. Both flagged the same prototype-pollution concern: `kind in equipmentCatalog` would match inherited `Object.prototype` keys (`toString`, `constructor`, etc.). Switched both equipment-kind checks (in `equipmentRequired` and `equipmentAlternatives`) to `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(equipmentCatalog, kind)`. Tests still pass (13/13 in `programSchema.spec.ts`); pushed as `d8fe56d` and replied to each comment.
